title: Open-Source Thermodynamic and Process Simulation as Computational Backbone for AI-Driven Integrated Production Optimization: Capabilities, Gaps, and a Development Roadmap
novelty_statement: First systematic assessment of open-source process simulation capabilities against the requirements of an AI-driven integrated production optimization framework, identifying specific software development needs and proposing a concrete roadmap for surrogate integration, RL environment wrapping, and reservoir-process coupling.

research_questions.question: What process simulation capabilities are required by an AI-driven integrated production optimization framework, and how well does the current open-source ecosystem (NeqSim) cover these requirements?
research_questions.method: Systematic requirements analysis mapped against existing capabilities with gap identification
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Complete requirements-capability matrix with quantitative coverage assessment

research_questions.question: What software architecture enables seamless integration of neural network surrogates within a physics-based process simulation framework while preserving thermodynamic consistency?
research_questions.method: Design of surrogate equipment interface, hybrid simulation patterns, and conservation enforcement
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Concrete interface specifications and pseudocode for surrogate-simulator coupling

research_questions.question: How can an open-source process simulator be wrapped as a reinforcement learning environment that supports multi-variable production optimization?
research_questions.method: Design of Gymnasium-compatible wrapper with observation/action/reward specification
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Complete RL environment specification with state/action space definitions

research_questions.question: What are the computational performance characteristics of the open-source simulator that determine the feasibility of surrogate training data generation and direct-simulation RL?
research_questions.method: Benchmarking of simulation execution times across representative process configurations
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Quantitative timing data with statistical characterization

sections: 2. Requirements for AI-Driven Integrated Production Optimization
3. Current Capabilities of the Open-Source Simulation Platform
5. Proposed Software Architecture for AI Integration
